samples.xml_description:
Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). Serial input data. Validity signal for input data. Parallel output data. Validity signal for the output data. 8 0 1 0 4-bit counter for counting the number of serial inputs. Valid signal for the output data. 8-bit parallel output data.

On each rising edge of the clock, if the reset signal is active (low), the counter cnt is reset to 0. Otherwise, if the input data is valid, the counter cnt is incremented by 1.

On the rising edge of the clock, if the reset signal is active (low), the valid signal is set to invalid (0). If the counter cnt equals 8, the valid signal is set to valid (1).

On the rising edge of the clock, if the reset signal is active (low), the parallel output data is set to 0. If the counter cnt equals 8, the parallel output data is updated with the latest values of dout_parallel.

When the counter cnt equals 8, the valid signal is valid (1), and the parallel output data is updated with the latest values of dout_parallel.

When the counter cnt does not equal 8, the valid signal is invalid (0), and the parallel output data is not updated.
